name: B.A. in Biology

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: The 8-term plan is well-ordered: a biology spine (BIOL 200 Concepts -> BIOL 201/202 Cell & Molecular and BIOL 205/206 Ecology & Evolution -> upper electives such as BIOL 222 Evolution and BIOL 352 Genetics), a chemistry track (CHEM 125/126 -> 243/244 Organic -> CHEM 473 Biochemistry), and algebra-based physics (PHYS 102/103) all run in parallel and support each other. Progression is smooth (deepestPrereqChain=5). The main softness is the number of generic 'Biology Elective'/'Cluster A/C Elective'/'Technical Elective' placeholders in years 3-4, which leave the upper-level path loosely specified.
dimensions.suggestion: Convert the repeated 'Biology Elective' and cluster placeholders into named example pathways (e.g., organismal, cell/molecular, ecology tracks) so students can see coherent upper-level routes.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: The prerequisite graph is healthy for a B.A.: deepestPrereqChain=5 is appropriate, and the 8 dangling references (missingCoursesSample: BIOL 202, BIOL 206, CHEM 125A, CHEM 126A, GER 200, GER 300, PHYS 102A, PHYS 103A) are all co-requisite lab components and general-education placeholders rather than genuine prerequisite breaks in the biology sequence.
dimensions.suggestion: Add catalog stubs mapping the co-requisite labs (BIOL 202/206, CHEM 125A/126A, PHYS 102A/103A) to their lecture courses so no referenced code dangles.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or educational objectives appear in the data; only the term plan and per-course descriptions are present. For a B.A. degree page this absence is a real finding.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish explicit program learning outcomes describing what a biology B.A. graduate can analyze, experiment with, and communicate.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: The plan reconciles exactly: statedTotalCredits=120, summedTermCredits=120, creditReconcileDelta=0. The distribution is a defensible liberal-arts split, with the science core front-loaded and generous free/technical electives and humanities GERs later, consistent with a B.A. rather than a B.S.
dimensions.suggestion: Add a brief credit-summary table (core vs. GER vs. elective totals) so the balance that already reconciles is visible at a glance.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: Terms are clearly labeled by year and semester and credits total to 120. Readability is only mildly undercut by null-code alternative rows ('or CHEM 121 or Fundamentals of Chemical Principles I') and several generic elective placeholders that read as blanks.
dimensions.suggestion: Render course alternatives as a single 'CHEM 125 or CHEM 121' line and give each elective placeholder an example course so the plan has no visually empty rows.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: The core is a sound classical biology education (evolution, genetics, physiology, ecology) with CHEM 473 Biochemistry and BNFO 135 Programming for Bioinformatics providing molecular and computational entry points. Currency gaps are that genetics (BIOL 352) and molecular content sit in the elective pool rather than the required core, quantitative training is only MATH 105 Elementary Probability and Statistics, and there is no required microbiology or genomics.
dimensions.suggestion: Require a molecular-biology/genetics course and strengthen quantitative training beyond MATH 105 so graduates leave with the data and genomics literacy expected in modern biology.

descriptionQuality.justification: Course descriptions are present and substantive across the spine (BIOL 200, BIOL 201, BIOL 205, CHEM 243/244, CHEM 473 Biochemistry), with only isolated gaps such as BIOL 352 Genetics carrying an empty excerpt.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: Prerequisites are stated with explicit grade conditions ('with a grade of C or better'), but clarity is reduced by pervasive legacy dual-numbering (e.g., 'BIOL 201 and BIOL 202 or R120 201 and R120 202'), which forces readers to reconcile two course-code systems, and by the dangling co-requisite labs.

missingTopics.rationale: Genetics (BIOL 352) and biochemistry (CHEM 473) exist but genetics sits among electives; a modern biology B.A. should require molecular/genomic content in the core.
Quantitative preparation is limited to MATH 105 Elementary Probability and Statistics, insufficient for reading and analyzing contemporary biological data.
No microbiology course appears in the required plan even though it is foundational to health, ecology, and biotechnology tracks students may pursue.
BNFO 135 Programming for Bioinformatics is a single intro; there is no follow-on course applying computation to sequence or 'omics data.

emergingTrends.rationale: Genetics is taught (BIOL 352) but the plan predates the routine use of genome editing and sequencing; a genomics elective would modernize the molecular track.
Building on BNFO 135, a biological-data-analysis course would give B.A. students the computational fluency now expected across the life sciences.
The ecology/evolution foundation (BIOL 205/222) positions the program to add a disease-ecology offering connecting environmental change to human and animal health.
With biochemistry (CHEM 473) and programming (BNFO 135) already present, a systems/synthetic-biology elective would connect the molecular and computational strands.

topSuggestions: Publish program-level learning outcomes; none currently appear on the page.
Require a molecular-biology/genetics course and strengthen quantitative training beyond MATH 105 to close the clearest currency gaps.
Convert the generic 'Biology Elective'/cluster placeholders into named example pathways so the upper-level route is legible.
Add a genomics/bioinformatics follow-on to BNFO 135 to give students computational-biology depth.
Add catalog stubs for the co-requisite labs (BIOL 202/206, CHEM 125A/126A, PHYS 102A/103A) so referenced codes resolve.
